title: Thailand Elevator and Escalator Market Size & Growth Forecast Report 2026-2031 Featuring Key vendors - Otis, KONE, TK Elevator, Schindler, Mitsubishi, Hitachi, Hyundai, and Fujitec
author: Researchandmarkets.Com
description: Thailand's elevator and escalator market is projected to grow at a CAGR of 3.20% from 2025 to 2031, driven by urbanization and commercial infrastructure expansion. The market is dominated by established players like Otis, KONE, and Mitsubishi, which collectively hold over 75% market share, though Chinese manufacturers are gaining ground. Despite favorable demand fundamentals, the market faces pricing pressures and competitive challenges.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Otis holds the strongest individual position with approximately 13% market value share and is recognized as a market leader. The company is also noted for recent digital advancements and innovation in the industry.
Schindler is part of the top six vendors and represents a well-established second tier of multinational OEMs, but no specific growth drivers or challenges are highlighted for the company.
Hitachi is among the top three vendors with strong competitive positioning in the Thai market, particularly in mid- to high-rise segments.
Fujitec is identified as part of the well-established second tier of multinational OEMs with no specific growth catalysts or challenges mentioned.
KONE is among the top three vendors with strong competitive positioning and is highlighted for digital advancements and innovation initiatives in the market.
Mitsubishi Electric is identified as one of the top three vendors with strong competitive intensity, particularly in mid- to high-rise commercial and residential segments.

title: Saudi Arabia Elevator and Escalator Market Size & Growth Forecast Report 2026-2031 Featuring Key Vendors - Otis, KONE, TK Elevator, Schindler, Mitsubishi, Hyundai, Hitachi, Fujitec
author: Researchandmarkets.Com
description: Saudi Arabia's elevator and escalator market is projected to grow at a CAGR of 3.73% from 2025 to 2031, driven by urbanization, infrastructure projects like Neom, and rising tourism. However, the market faces headwinds from skilled labor shortages and rising construction costs. Top vendors Otis, KONE, and Schindler collectively control approximately 75% of the market.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Otis is identified as a top-5 vendor controlling ~75% of market share collectively with peers. The company is implementing AI-based predictive maintenance solutions and positioned to benefit from 3.73% CAGR growth driven by urbanization and major infrastructure projects.
Schindler is among the top-5 vendors controlling ~75% of market share. Benefits from strong growth drivers including urbanization, tourism surge, and major infrastructure projects like Neom.
Fujitec is mentioned as a key vendor but holds a smaller market share than top-5 players. Limited specific details provided about company-specific opportunities or challenges.
Mitsubishi is a top-5 vendor with significant market share in Saudi Arabia's growing elevator and escalator market, positioned to benefit from 3.73% CAGR growth through 2031.
Hitachi is among the top-5 vendors with substantial market share. Benefits from strong market fundamentals including urbanization, tourism growth, and infrastructure development.

title: Vertiv: The Backlog is Massive, the Margin of Safety is Not
author: Gurufocus
description: Vertiv (VRT), a leader in data center power and thermal infrastructure for AI, has surged 300% in 12 months with a massive $15 billion backlog and improving unit economics. However, the stock trades at a 50.9x forward P/E multiple with only a 1.9% FCF yield, leaving minimal margin of safety despite strong fundamentals and growth prospects. The valuation assumes sustained high growth and successful services transformation, with execution risks from project delays and financing constraints.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong business fundamentals with 28% YoY revenue growth, 20.4% operating margins, and structural positioning in AI infrastructure. However, valuation at 50.9x forward P/E is stretched with minimal margin of safety. The article acknowledges exceptional business quality but advises against adding at current levels due to limited downside protection.
Mentioned as a major hyperscaler with $650B+ collective capex guidance for 2026 alongside Amazon, Meta, and Microsoft, supporting strong demand for Vertiv's infrastructure products.
Mentioned as a major hyperscaler with $650B+ collective capex guidance for 2026 alongside Amazon, Meta, and Microsoft, supporting strong demand for Vertiv's infrastructure products.
Major hyperscaler with significant capex commitments supporting AI infrastructure buildout and demand for Vertiv's solutions.
Key hyperscaler with substantial capex guidance supporting the AI infrastructure cycle that benefits Vertiv.
Major hyperscaler with significant capex commitments driving demand for data center power and cooling infrastructure.
Vertiv has deep ties with NVIDIA, providing early visibility into hardware roadmaps and engineering solutions in parallel with new GPU platforms, creating structural competitive advantage.
Direct competitor in power management and thermal infrastructure benefiting from data center buildout, but growing 4.4x slower than Vertiv with lower valuation multiples (28x forward P/E).
Competitor in power management benefiting from same data center cycle but growing 2.6x slower than Vertiv with lower valuation (30.5x forward P/E).
Peer company in thermal infrastructure with lower valuation multiple (32.1x forward P/E) and slower growth compared to Vertiv.

title: Otis Announces 5 Percent Increase in Quarterly Dividend to $0.44 per Share
author: Prnewswire
description: Otis Worldwide Corporation announced a 5% increase in its quarterly dividend to $0.44 per share, payable on June 12, 2026. CEO Judy Marks attributed the increase to the strength of the company's service-driven business and strong cash flows, noting that the dividend has grown approximately 120% since the company's 2020 spin-off.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company announced a 5% dividend increase, demonstrating strong financial performance and cash generation from its service-driven business. The 120% dividend growth since 2020 and management's confidence in sustainable returns to shareholders indicate solid business fundamentals and positive outlook.

title: Otis Declares Quarterly Dividend of $0.42 per Share
author: Prnewswire
description: Otis Worldwide Corporation announced a quarterly dividend of $0.42 per share, payable on March 13, 2026, to shareholders of record as of February 13, 2026. The global elevator and escalator manufacturer serves 2.5 billion people daily through its 2.5 million customer units worldwide.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company declared a quarterly dividend of $0.42 per share, demonstrating financial strength and commitment to returning capital to shareholders. Dividend declarations are typically viewed positively as they indicate confidence in the company's cash flow generation and financial stability.
